mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously as long as the rate trajectory is downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.7% as of 2023-11-15 suggests easing cycle underway
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, it signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% as of 2023-11-15 remains near historical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite unemployment deterioration signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) above its 12-month moving average combined with housing starts (HOUST) above trend indicates robust consumer demand and housing investment, signaling sustained economic expansion that drives positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS > 12-month MA AND HOUST > 1200k units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 501971M and HOUST at 1287k as of 2023-11-15
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window despite both demand signals being active.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines by more than 1.5% over a 3-month rolling window, signaling supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk that compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite industrial production decline, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains above 3.5% while the federal funds rate is below 2.5%, the real policy rate is restrictive but the gap is narrowing, signaling an end to the tightening cycle. This regime transition produces positive equity returns as investors anticipate rate cuts over the next 60 days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 250.535 (approx 3.4% YoY) and FEDFUNDS at 1.7% as of 2023-11-15
regime_hint: transition from monetary tightening to easing cycle
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns when real rate gap narrows below 2.0pp.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100k new jobs per month over a 3-month rolling average, signaling labor market softening that precedes broader economic weakness and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as growth expectations compress.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market deceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite payroll deceleration, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized rate while simultaneously retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both exceed their respective 12-month moving averages, indicating broad-based demand expansion that drives positive equity returns over 60 trading days through multiple growth channels.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized AND RSAFS > 12-month MA AND HOUST > 12-month MA
regime_hint: broad-based economic expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances despite all three demand indicators signaling expansion, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above its 12-month moving average while unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains stable or declines, indicating supply-side expansion without labor market stress. This regime produces positive equity returns over 20 trading days as productivity gains support profit margins.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO > 12-month MA AND UNRATE change < 0.1pp in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.3006 and UNRATE at 3.8% as of 2023-11-15
regime_hint: productivity-driven expansion with stable labor market
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances despite supply acceleration and labor stability, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
